title: humboltia recurva
parts: Humboltia recurva has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and flowers, which are utilized in traditional medicine. The leaves of Humboltia recurva contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of this plant are rich in sesquiterpenoids and glycosides, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and hepatoprotective activities. The flowers of Humboltia recurva contain anthocyanins and carotenoids, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
side_effects: Humboltia recurva can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high toxic alkaloid content. If ingested in large quantities, it can cause cardiovascular problems, including irregular heartbeat and increased blood pressure. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to skin irritation, rashes, and allergic reactions in some individuals. In severe cases, improper use of Humboltia recurva can even cause neurological symptoms, including seizures and coma.
